methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $169M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies, a Series F climate and energy company founded by Robert Niven, has raised $169M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two primary methods triangulated against a peer set of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable ( Linnea Oil, LNZA), including same-stage and adjacent-stage peers such as Helion (Series F, $5.4B), TAE Technologies (Series G, $6.0B), Form Energy (Series G, $3.5B), and Watershed (Series G, $1.8B).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier to the $169M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $220M. The peer-median method across the four primary comparables yields $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.8B to $6.0B. These two approaches produce a wide spread reflecting both the early-stage capital base and the heterogeneous outcomes observed in the climate-tech peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $187M (low) to $5.1B (high), with a median estimate of $2.3B. The substantial width of this range reflects material uncertainty around CarbonCure's scaling trajectory relative to peers. Key variables include commercialization velocity, competitive positioning within concrete and carbon utilization, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set. Further diligence on revenue traction and unit economics would narrow this range materially.
